label: Linear
description: Connect to Linear to read and manage issues, projects, and cycles for your engineering team.

form.title: Connect Linear
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to access your Linear workspace via a personal API key.

methods.label: In-Browser Connect
methods.description: Opens Linear sign-in in your browser. No credentials required — just click Connect.

methods.label: Personal API Key
methods.description: Paste a Linear personal API key generated from Settings → API → Personal API keys. Quickest path — no OAuth app setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Linear Personal API Key

Linear personal API keys give scoped access to your workspace without requiring an OAuth app setup.

### Steps

1. Open [Linear](https://linear.app) and sign in.
2. Click your workspace avatar in the top-left, then go to **Settings**.
3. In the left sidebar, click **API** under the **My Account** section.
4. Scroll to **Personal API keys** and click **Create key**.
5. Give the key a name like `Anton` so you can identify it later.
6. Click **Create** — Linear shows the key **once**. Copy it immediately.
7. Paste the key into the **API Key** field below.

### Notes

- The key inherits your Linear account permissions — it can access all teams and projects you belong to.
- If you lose the key, delete it in Settings → API and generate a new one.
- Keys can be revoked at any time from the same API settings page without affecting other integrations.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Your Linear personal API key. Found in Settings → API → Personal API keys. Shown only once when created.

fields.name: workspace_url
fields.label: Workspace URL
fields.description: Optional. Your Linear workspace URL — helps Anton disambiguate if you belong to multiple workspaces.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (BYOK)
methods.description: Use a Linear OAuth application you create in your workspace settings. More setup, but better suited for team-wide or multi-user deployments.
methods.how_to:
## OAuth 2.0 — Bring Your Own OAuth App

This method requires creating a Linear OAuth application in your workspace. Suitable for deployments where multiple users need to connect.

### Steps

1. Open [Linear](https://linear.app) and sign in as a workspace admin.
2. Go to **Settings** → **API** → **OAuth applications**.
3. Click **Create application**.
4. Set the name to `Anton` and add `http://127.0.0.1:47289/callback` as a redirect URI (Linear requires an exact match, including port).
5. Click **Create** — Linear shows your **Client ID** and **Client Secret**.
6. Copy both values into the fields below.
7. Click **Submit** — Anton opens a browser window for you to authorize the app.

### Scopes requested

- `read` — read all workspace data
- `write` — create and update issues and comments
- `issues:create` — create new issues
- `comments:create` — post comments

### Notes

- The client secret is shown once; store it securely.
- You can revoke the OAuth app at any time from Settings → API → OAuth applications.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: From Linear Settings → API → OAuth applications → your app's Client ID.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Paired with the Client ID. Shown once when the OAuth app is created in Linear.
